title: Mobile responsive: modals (voice picker, About, info panels)
description: Voice picker modal, About modal, and inline info panels are currently fixed-width and overflow on phone. Make them fit and be tappable at 390px. Part of meditation-pal-leo.

title: Validate native iOS/Android STT for meditation use
description:
Pilot `SFSpeechRecognizer` (iOS) and Android `SpeechRecognizer` via a Capacitor plugin to confirm they handle meditation-specific speech patterns before committing to the no-Whisper-on-mobile strategy.

Test cases that may break native APIs (which are tuned for Siri-style queries):
- **Long pauses** without the recognizer giving up / cutting off
- **Soft, whispered, or quiet speech** (relevant during deep meditation)
- **Brief 1–2 word noting utterances** ('warmth', 'tightness', 'sound')
- **Half-finished sentences** trailing into pauses
- **iOS aggressive silence cutoff** — defaults to ~1.5s. Configurable?

Deliverable: a quick prototype Capacitor app or test page that runs the speech recognizer with realistic meditation input. Decision output: do we go native, or do we need a cloud-Whisper fallback path for mobile?

If native fails: streaming audio to a hosted Whisper endpoint is still cheaper than bundling the model. Document the fallback architecture if needed.

Spawned from 2026-05-09 mobile architecture conversation.

title: App icon from favicon for .app bundle
Mobile nav skeleton: bottom tab bar + breakpoint foundation
description: Convert top nav to a bottom tab bar at small breakpoints (target: <768px). Establish the mobile breakpoint convention (CSS custom props, single source of truth) that the rest of the responsive work builds on. About/theme/fullscreen buttons stay accessible (overflow menu or compact top bar). Foundation for the other mobile sub-issues. Part of meditation-pal-leo.

title: Validate on-device LLM quality for facilitator role
description:
Pilot small on-device LLMs to confirm they can hold up as the default mode for the standalone mobile app. The 'on-device by default, your meditations never leave your phone' pitch only works if the on-device experience is genuinely good — not a crippled trial pushing users toward the paid cloud tier.

Candidates to test:
- **Apple Intelligence Foundation Models** (iOS 18+ on A17 Pro / M-series). Free, on-device, no bundling. Most attractive if quality holds.
- **Gemma 2/3 (2B–9B)** via MLC-LLM or llama.cpp (mobile builds exist)
- **Phi-3 / Phi-4 mini**
- **Llama 3.2 (1B / 3B)**

Quality bar: must handle the practices that distinguish aloud. — not just generic supportive presence.
- Body / emotions / parts work prompts
- Directiveness slider responsiveness (especially mid-to-high)
- Recognition of subtle states (jhana factors, somatic shifts)
- The `[HOLD]` signal behavior (silence mode entry)
- Coherent multi-turn over a 20–40 minute session
- Doesn't drift into ChatGPT-as-monk vibes

Deliverable: model recommendation, a 'this is the on-device experience' demo session, and an honest writeup of where it falls short relative to Sonnet — those gaps inform the cloud-upgrade pitch.

Spawned from 2026-05-09 mobile architecture conversation. Blocks dbd's design (default tier viability).

title: Service worker & offline shell
description: Add service worker for PWA offline shell caching (app shell, CSS, JS). Not full offline — LLM/TTS need network — but the UI should load instantly from cache. Part of PWA requirements for Add to Home Screen.

title: Live credit-use meter / cost transparency in session UI
description:
Show users their compute spend in real time / per session — e.g. a small live meter during a session and a 'this session used ~2.3 cents of compute' summary after. Radical-transparency UX that reinforces the project's values guardrail (meditation-pal-a2j: 'you're buying compute, not calm'): most apps hide unit cost; surfacing it builds trust instead of spending it, and it fits the aloud. brand.

Scope:
- Live credit/cost readout during a session (debits as LLM tokens + STT minutes + TTS chars accrue).
- Post-session summary of spend.
- Works for both metered credit users and (informationally) BYOK/free users.
- Honest rounding; don't make the meter anxiety-inducing — calm, ambient, optional to expand.

Depends on the metered billing engine (meditation-pal-8sj) for the underlying per-request cost data. Relates to meditation-pal-a2j (values/copy). Child of the web demo epic but applies to mobile too.

title: TS UI: backlog of new ideas to revisit after parity
description:
Backlog of ideas that emerged during the TS port. Per the lift-first rule, these are deferred until the app reaches 1:1 parity with the existing Flask-served UI. User decides which to revisit when.

- Multi-state orb (listening / thinking / speaking / holding visual modes during a session)
- Engine-grouped voice picker with optgroups
- Server-Whisper VAD currently does naive single-pass capture; existing audio.js has speculative submission, pre-buffer, barge-in. Port verbatim first, then revisit any improvements.
- 'Re-probe Flask backend on each session start' added to STT picker — keep
- Anything else flagged with TODO(lift-first) comments in /ts/ source

title: Document LAN setup for friends
description: README or quick-start guide covering: network requirements, accepting self-signed certs, supported browsers.

title: Server-side TTS for web (Piper or ElevenLabs) behind tts-picker
description: Decide and wire the web demo's voice. browser-tts.ts (speechSynthesis) is free/client-side but voice quality varies by device/OS ('crap voice' risk). For 'non-crap' consistent voice, add a server TTS path: Piper (free, CPU on our box) as default, ElevenLabs (per-char, metered) as premium/credit-spend option. Slot behind ts/ui/src/adapters/tts-picker.ts alongside server-tts.ts. ElevenLabs cost feeds the metered billing engine. Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.

title: Add Groq as an LLM provider
description: OpenAI-compatible endpoint at api.groq.com/openai/v1. Free tier + paid. Wire into PROVIDERS registry, config env map, provider_routes (availability + model fetch), and frontend (index/settings/tour). Default model: llama-3.3-70b-versatile.

title: Port facilitation engine to TypeScript
description: Port the core facilitation logic from Python to TypeScript. This is the soul of the app. Includes: PromptBuilder (prompts.py - 482 lines of composable system prompt construction with focus overlays, quality modifiers, directiveness dial, custom instructions), SessionManager (context strategy, rolling window, message history), PacingController (6 conversation states, 4 turn decisions, silence detection thresholds, contemplative pause logic), and [HOLD]/[HOLD?] signal detection. Must preserve exact prompt text and pacing timing values.

title: TS rewrite: orchestration core
description:
Port the platform-agnostic orchestration layer from src/facilitation + src/llm to TypeScript so it can run inside Capacitor as JS on the device.

Scaffold landed on branch 'ts-core' (2026-05-14):
- /ts/ package (TypeScript + Vitest)
- Ported: PacingController, SessionManager (28 tests pass)
- Pending: PromptBuilder (largest piece — ~530 lines of composable system prompts), LLMProvider interface + Anthropic implementation, ollama provider, [HOLD] signal parsing
- Then: STT/TTS platform abstraction (interfaces only — native impls land in Capacitor work)
- Then: storage abstraction (browser localStorage / Capacitor Preferences)

When the TS surface covers what's needed for mobile, the Capacitor wrapper (meditation-pal-3k5) can wrap it. Python backend continues to run desktop until/unless we migrate desktop too.

title: Free-tier gating + abuse/quota controls
description: Give each verified account 1-2 free sessions on the CHEAPEST model (so burner-account abuse is cheap to absorb). Layer IP/device heuristics and rate-limits for determined abusers; accept some leakage. This is load-bearing for the web demo specifically because — unlike mobile (gated by app-store distribution) — the web is open to the internet, so free sessions are an unguarded cost faucet. Lives in/with the standalone proxy. Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.

title: Mobile responsive: index page setup form
description: Make the / setup form usable on phones. Stack form-row/form-row-thirds vertically, full-width style cards and modifier toggles, larger tap targets, scrollable. Demote less-essential controls behind the existing <details class='advanced-settings'> — minimal essentials by default, 'detailed settings' expands the rest. Target: iPhone 13 (390px) minimum. Part of meditation-pal-leo.

title: Capacitor native wrapper for app stores
description:
Wrap the web app with Capacitor for iOS App Store and Google Play submission.

Architecture decisions (2026-05-09):
- **STT**: use native iOS `SFSpeechRecognizer` and Android `SpeechRecognizer` via a Capacitor plugin (e.g. `@capacitor-community/speech-recognition`). Do NOT bundle Whisper on mobile — strips ~150MB+ from the app and avoids battery/memory pain. Quality validation needed first (see meditation-specific risks ticket).
- **TTS**: use native `AVSpeechSynthesizer` (iOS) / `TextToSpeech` (Android) by default; ElevenLabs as premium upgrade. Drop Piper bundling on mobile.
- **LLM**: tiered (on-device small model / BYOK / hosted cloud) — see meditation-pal-dbd.
- **Backend**: open question whether to keep Python (server runs separately, mobile talks to hosted endpoint) or rewrite in TypeScript so it can run inside Capacitor as JS — see backend rewrite decision ticket.
- **Privacy disclosure**: be transparent if native STT falls back to Apple/Google cloud; meditators care a lot about their words not leaving the device.

Needs responsive layout (done), PWA, mobile audio quirks done first.

title: Add HTTPS via reverse proxy
description: Browsers block getUserMedia() on non-localhost HTTP. Set up Caddy (or nginx) as a reverse proxy with TLS. Document self-signed cert workflow for LAN use.

title: Voice quality onboarding: prompt, Piper bundling, dev debug flags
App store preparation & polish
description: Final polish for app store submission. Includes: app icon and splash screen, App Store / Play Store metadata (screenshots, description, privacy policy), handle app review requirements (explain microphone usage, explain API key storage), dark/light theme support matching web app, accessibility (VoiceOver/TalkBack compatibility - important for a voice app), haptics, loading states, error handling for network failures.

title: Build Session screen (mobile)
description: Port the web session page to a React Native screen. Core elements: conversation display (scrolling list of user/assistant turns), mic/record button with visual feedback (listening state), session timer, TTS on/off toggle, speaking rate slider, end session button, silence/hold mode indicator. Must handle: keep screen awake during session, background audio session (so TTS plays even if screen locks), haptic feedback on state changes. The session screen is where users spend 95% of their time - it needs to feel seamless.

title: Haptic feedback on noting tab
description:
When a participant's turn fires in a noting session, trigger a subtle navigator.vibrate() pulse on supported platforms (Android Chrome, primarily — iOS Safari does not support the Vibration API).

Idea source (end-of-day 2026-05-09 brainstorm with Claude): less audio-busy alternative to the card sound. Could be opt-in alongside or instead of the existing user-turn-cue sound.

Design notes:
- Probably a new toggle next to 'Play a sound when it's your turn' — or replace that toggle with a 3-way: silent / sound / vibrate.
- Vibration pattern: short single pulse (~50ms) feels right for a 'your turn' cue. Test on actual phones.
- Gracefully no-op on iOS Safari and any platform without navigator.vibrate. Don't surface the option if not supported.

title: App Store & Play Store submission
description: Signing, app store listings, screenshots, review process for iOS and Android. Final step in mobile distribution pipeline.

title: Pre-launch: App Store AGPL license-grant exception
description:
Before submitting to the iOS App Store, publish a copyright-holder exception that grants Apple the right to distribute the binary under their standard terms.

**Why this is needed**: Apple's App Store Usage Rules (device-count limits, FairPlay DRM, no redistribution) impose restrictions that AGPL §6 forbids ('no further restrictions'). VLC famously got pulled from iOS in 2011 over this exact conflict. The FSF's position is that pure AGPL software cannot legally be distributed via the App Store unless the copyright holder grants an explicit additional permission.

**The fix**: as the copyright holder, add a `LICENSE-EXCEPTION.md` (or equivalent section in README/LICENSE) granting Apple a non-exclusive license to distribute under their terms — AGPL still applies to the source.

Example wording (adjust with legal review):
> The copyright holder additionally grants Apple, Inc. and any party operating under its terms a non-exclusive permission to distribute, link, and combine this software with Apple proprietary frameworks under Apple's standard App Store distribution terms, notwithstanding the restrictions of AGPL §6.

**Contributor caveat**: this only works cleanly if the user is sole copyright holder. Once external contributors gain copyright, they'd need to agree too — easiest pre-launch fix is to add a CONTRIBUTING.md note that submitting code is acknowledgment of the App Store exception, before accepting non-trivial PRs.

Google Play has no equivalent restriction, so this is iOS-only.

Spawned from 2026-05-09 license review. Should land before any App Store submission (related: meditation-pal-7rh).

title: Run behind production WSGI server
description: Replace Flask dev server with gunicorn + eventlet (or gevent) for concurrent Socket.IO connections.

title: Cross-platform mobile app (React Native / Expo)
description: Epic: Build a cross-platform iOS/Android app with the same meditation facilitation functionality as the web app. Use React Native with Expo for shared codebase. The app calls LLM APIs directly, uses platform STT/TTS, and ports the facilitation logic (prompt builder, pacing, hold detection) to TypeScript.

title: Bind server to 0.0.0.0 for LAN access
description: Flask and/or Socket.IO likely bind to 127.0.0.1. Change to 0.0.0.0 so LAN clients can connect. LLM proxy on 8317 can stay localhost if only used server-side.

title: Credits + metered billing backend (Stripe) [spec-first]
description: SPEC FIRST, then implement. Design the credits engine for the web demo, then build it. Core decision: price each session by METERED actual cost (LLM tokens + STT minutes + TTS chars) x margin multiplier (e.g. 1.5-2x), debited from a credit balance with a small pre-auth hold at session start. Metered debit is the direct fix for the cost-math trap meditation-pal-a2j flags (a daily power user can't underwater a flat sub). Stripe for payments (~3%, vs mobile's 30% store tax). Cover: credit-pack price points, the metered formula + margin, Stripe integration, sales-tax/VAT (Stripe Tax), chargebacks, and ToS for reselling LLM/voice access. Extends meditation-pal-a2j. Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.

title: Settings page overflows at larger text-scale settings
description:
The settings page has several px-based responsive breakpoints (e.g. 475px for the Save Settings wrap, 450px for the footer secondary buttons, 530px for various stack-into-column rules). When the user increases the in-app text scale, the actual content widens but the breakpoints don't shift, so layouts overflow before the next breakpoint fires.

Likely fix: convert the settings-page breakpoints from px to em so they scale with the root font-size (and the in-app text-scale setting, if it's wired to rem/root size). Container queries are a heavier alternative for components whose own width matters more than the viewport.

Low priority — only noticeable at significantly bumped text sizes.

title: Add info modals to exploration and noting pages explaining the practices
description: Add a question mark (?) button on the exploration and noting pages that opens a modal with 1-2 paragraphs explaining why the practices are worthwhile and what mindset to bring to them.

title: Mic tuning UI: device picker, live input meter, barge-in sensitivity
description:
Follow-up to the echo-cancellation fix (echoCancellation/noiseSuppression/autoGainControl now set in getUserMedia, audio.js). For rooms/devices where AEC isn't enough:
- Device picker via enumerateDevices() (currently no way to choose the input mic).
- Live input-level meter so users SEE their voice cross a threshold vs idle noise/echo (better than a blind sensitivity slider).
- Barge-in sensitivity control; consider an adaptive threshold that rises relative to the measured echo/noise floor while TTS is playing (extend the existing VAD adaptive-noise-floor concept).
- Surface in a small in-session gear modal AND on the settings page.
- Gentle 'headphones eliminate echo' hint for speaker users.
- Advanced/maybe-skip: detect mic hearing TTS's own words (compare transcription to TTS text) and back off.
Read dev-docs/voice-barge-in.md before touching barge-in logic. Triggered by a tester whose facilitator interrupted itself (echo tripping barge-in).

title: Lower pywebview min_size to modern-iPhone dimensions
description: Once mobile responsive layout work is done, lower the pywebview min_size from (1024, 600) to something corresponding to a modern iPhone — proposed (390, 700) so the app works at iPhone-13-class dimensions but isn't silly small. Verify the native window still works on macOS/Windows/Linux at the new minimum. Part of meditation-pal-leo.

title: Mobile pricing & monetization spec
description:
Lock in the pricing structure for the standalone mobile app. Goals: accessible price, sustainable economics, no values compromise (no 'pay monthly to feel calm' framing).

Proposed structure (from 2026-05-09 design conversation):
- **App purchase**: ~$7.99 one-time ($5 instinct adjusted for inflation; below $9.99 'real app' threshold but above 'trinket' $4.99)
- **On-device LLM**: free, full functionality, indefinite — the real default
- **BYOK**: free tier, user's own Anthropic/OpenAI/OpenRouter key
- **Hosted cloud**: optional upgrade
  - Pack: ~$4.99 / ~100 sessions (one-time, no recurring)
  - Subscription: ~$3.99/month unlimited (framed as 'we host the model for you', not 'meditation premium')
- **Welcome credit**: app purchase includes a small batch (e.g. 10) of cloud sessions to try the high-quality tier — onboarding hook without subscription pressure

Open math problem:
- Sonnet at ~$0.05–0.20 per 30-min session can underwater the $3.99/mo tier for daily users (30 sessions × $0.20 = $6 cost > $3.99 revenue). Solutions: cheaper default model on the hosted tier (Haiku / Sonnet small / open-weights on a cheap inference provider), soft monthly cap, or reprice.
- Apple/Google take 30% (15% for small business). Net economics need to work after that cut.

Deliverable: a spreadsheet covering median + heavy-user economics, final price points, and an honest copy spec for how the tiers are presented in-app and on the marketing page.

Related: dbd (cloud LLM architecture), toz (web page that may communicate pricing).

Cost lever (added 2026-05-25 by Claude): prompt caching materially changes the per-session math above. The system prompt is currently cached at the default 5-min ephemeral TTL, but sessions have long silent gaps (the 300s check-in is a canned phrase, not an LLM call), so the cache frequently expires between turns and each call repays a ~1.25x cache write instead of a ~0.1x read. A 1h (or adaptive) TTL keeps the prefix warm across pauses and lowers hosted-tier cost. See meditation-pal-ttl for analysis and options. Note: only the direct Anthropic API path can set TTL — the claude-proxy/CLI subscription path cannot.

title: Deploy: ui/dist static host + standalone proxy on Fly/Render (HTTPS)
description: Infra for the web demo. Frontend: ui/dist (Vite build) is static — host on the existing static host (e.g. GitHub Pages) or any CDN. Backend: the standalone proxy on a small always-on VM (Fly ~$5-10/mo, Render Starter $7, or a cheap VPS); NOT AWS-required and cheaper/simpler than AWS at trial scale. Real TLS (mic needs a secure context; cert.py self-signed is LAN-only). Single process is fine at trial scale; note Redis/sticky-sessions only matter if we later make the backend stateful. Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.

title: API key management & config (mobile)
description: Secure storage for API keys (Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenRouter, ElevenLabs) using expo-secure-store. Settings screen for: choosing LLM provider and model, entering API keys, selecting TTS engine and voice, default speaking rate, default verbosity. Persist all config with AsyncStorage. First-run onboarding flow to set up at least one LLM provider.

title: Cloud-STT endpoint for web (Groq Whisper); decouple server-whisper-stt from Flask
description:
ts/ui/src/adapters/server-whisper-stt.ts currently posts PCM to Flask's /api/stt/whisper. For a hosted demo that can't depend on someone's local Flask+Whisper. Add a cloud-STT path: forward captured audio to Groq Whisper Large v3 Turbo (~$0.04/hr audio) via the standalone backend, and repoint the adapter/endpoint accordingly.

Note (cost): meditation is mostly silence (VAD-gated), so billed audio is small — well under a cent per session even with overhead. Relates to meditation-pal-0ao (native STT validation) which already notes cloud-Whisper fallback as the documented escape hatch. Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.

title: Mobile responsive: settings page
description: Make /settings usable on phones. Stack form-row form-group-half pairs, mobile-friendly section spacing, ensure all the provider/TTS controls work at 390px. Mostly mechanical. Part of meditation-pal-leo.

title: Port audio.js VAD niceties (speculative / adaptive silence / pre-buffer) to TS
description: ts/ui/src/adapters/server-whisper-stt.ts has an intentionally simple RMS+silence-timeout VAD; its header says the Flask UI's audio.js niceties (speculative submission, adaptive silence thresholding, pre-buffer for utterance onset) should be ported 'when the loop is solid'. Port them so the web demo's turn-taking matches the Python pipeline's snappiness. Reference: src/web/static/js/audio.js (submitSpeculative/finalizeSpeculative, adaptive silenceNeeded ramp, preBuffer). Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.

title: Web demo of aloud. (hosted, accounts + credits)
description:
Umbrella for making aloud. accessible as a hosted web demo (distinct from the marketing page in meditation-pal-toz). A stranger hits a URL, signs in, gets 1-2 free sessions on a cheap/on-device-class model, then buys credits to access premium LLMs and voices, with margin built in.

Key architectural reframe (2026-05-23 design conversation): the web demo is a DEPLOYMENT TARGET of the TS rewrite (meditation-pal-2qg), not a separate build. The ts/ui Vite build (ui/dist) is static and can sit on the existing static host (e.g. GitHub Pages); only a backend proxy needs a server. The web STT/TTS/LLM adapters already exist under ts/ui/src/adapters (web-speech-stt, server-whisper-stt, server-tts, claude-proxy-http) but currently point at the desktop Flask backend — the web demo must decouple them onto a standalone hosted backend.

Relationship to mobile plans: this REUSES meditation-pal-dbd (cloud LLM proxy) and meditation-pal-a2j (pricing/monetization). The web variant differs from mobile in three ways worth a fresh spec: (1) no 30% app-store tax — Stripe ~3%, roughly doubling margin headroom and softening a2j's cost-math trap; (2) auth is web OAuth (Google/Apple/etc.) not IAP; (3) abuse surface is wide open (no app-store gating), so free-tier quota/abuse controls are load-bearing.

Blocked by the TS rewrite reaching browser-usable parity (meditation-pal-2qg).

title: API key UX: Get-key link + paste-from-clipboard for all providers
description: Add per-input action row: 'Get a key' opens provider console in new tab; 'Paste from clipboard' uses navigator.clipboard.readText (validates against key prefix). Covers anthropic/openai/groq/openrouter/venice + elevenlabs. Highlights paste button when user returns from console tab.

title: TS UI: revisit rewrites under lift-first rule
description:
Per the lift-first rule (saved 2026-05-14): default to 1:1 port of existing UI behavior, save new ideas as separate notes. Several pieces of the TS preview were written from scratch rather than lifted; redo them to match the existing app.

Pieces to revisit:
- Session orb — existing animations live in src/web/static/css/. Currently I shipped multi-state breathing/listening/thinking/speaking/holding modes; existing orb has its own behavior with kasina mode, drag-to-position, rainbow easter egg, etc.
- Theme toggle — port chrome.js theme block more directly (icon SVGs, 8-clicks-in-4s easter egg)
- Voice picker — existing voice-picker.js + _voice_modal.html has modal UI with previews, speed slider, voice categorization, 'needs download' state. Current TS preview has a basic dropdown.
- General CSS — large parts of src/web/static/css/ haven't been lifted; my own CSS is fine for layout but loses visual identity

After lifting, retain quality but stage refactors / new ideas in a separate backlog ticket so the user can decide which to keep.

title: Cross-platform credits: sell on web (Stripe), spend on mobile — bypass IAP cut
description:
STRATEGY + SPEC. Make credits a portable account-bound currency so users can BUY them on the web store (Stripe, ~3-5% all-in) and SPEND them in the mobile app — instead of buying via Apple/Google IAP and losing 15-30%. This is the 'Pokemon Go / Fortnite web store' model and is potentially the single biggest lever on the economics meditation-pal-a2j worries about (a2j assumes a 30% store tax that this largely removes).

Why it's newly viable (verified 2026-05, but UNSETTLED — re-check before building):
- US: since the Apr/May-2025 Epic v. Apple contempt ruling, App Store apps may link out to external web purchases with NO Apple commission and no entitlement. Dec-2025 Ninth Circuit largely upheld this.
- RISK: the Ninth Circuit said Apple MAY set a 'reasonable commission' on externally-linked purchases; the framework is still being decided on remand and Apple is seeking Supreme Court review (rehearing denied Mar-2026, remand proceeding Apr-2026). So US ~0% commission is real today but could change.
- EU: NOT free — External Purchase Link entitlement required and Core Technology Commission (~12-20%) applies. Model EU separately.
- You take on payment processing, sales tax/VAT, fraud, and compliance for web-sold credits.

Design notes:
- Credits must be account-bound (requires the OAuth/account model, meditation-pal-rfb) and balance-checked server-side by the proxy so they're spendable cross-device.
- The mobile app links out to the web checkout (compliant external link), user returns and balance reflects the top-up.
- Keep an IAP path as fallback for users who won't leave the app / for jurisdictions where external links are restricted; accept the cut there.

Depends on the credits/billing engine (meditation-pal-8sj) and accounts (meditation-pal-rfb). Extends meditation-pal-a2j. Child of the web demo epic; serves mobile economics.

title: Cloud LLM proxy for standalone mobile
description:
Server-side LLM access for the standalone mobile app, structured as a tiered offering rather than a single proxy.

Tiers (per 2026-05-09 design conversation):
- **Free / on-device**: small LLM running locally (Apple Intelligence Foundation Models on iOS 18+, Gemma/Phi/Llama 3.2 via MLC-LLM or llama.cpp on Android). Always available, no network, full privacy. Default mode.
- **BYOK**: user supplies their own Anthropic/OpenAI/OpenRouter key. Free tier — power users who want best quality at their own cost.
- **Hosted cloud**: paid access to a curated high-quality model. Two purchase shapes:
  - Session pack: ~$4.99 for ~100 sessions (one-time, no recurring)
  - Subscription: ~$3.99/month unlimited (clearly framed as paying for compute, not for meditation content)

Open questions / TODOs:
- Cost math: Sonnet at ~$0.05–0.20 per 30-min session could underwater the $3.99/mo tier for heavy users. Either choose a cheaper default model (Haiku, Sonnet 4 small, etc.), put a soft cap, or reprice. Needs spreadsheet pass before commit.
- Hosted backend stack — keep Python Flask, or rewrite in TypeScript so the mobile + desktop stories converge? See separate decision ticket.
- Abuse / quota / auth model for hosted tier (per-user keys, sign-in with Apple/Google).
- Transparency copy: ensure the on-device tier is honestly framed as 'real and complete', not crippleware.

Phase 1 (phone-as-remote, jim) doesn't need any of this — it talks to the user's own desktop. This ticket is for Phase 2 (standalone install).

Cost lever (added 2026-05-25 by Claude): prompt-cache TTL is a direct knob on the hosted-tier cost math above. The default 5-min ephemeral cache on the system prompt dies during meditation's long silent gaps, so calls repay cache writes (~1.25x) rather than reads (~0.1x). A 1h or adaptive TTL keeps the prefix warm. See meditation-pal-ttl. Caveats: TTL control exists only on the direct Anthropic API provider, not the claude-proxy/CLI path; and today only the system prompt is cached (not the growing conversation), so confirm the rendered prompt clears the model's min-cacheable size (~2048 tokens Sonnet 4.6 / 4096 Opus) before relying on it.

title: Implement text-to-speech (mobile)
description: Implement TTS for meditation responses. Options: (1) expo-speech - wraps platform TTS (AVSpeechSynthesizer on iOS, Android TTS) - free, offline, decent quality. (2) ElevenLabs API - highest quality, costs money, requires network. Start with expo-speech (platform voices). Must handle: configurable speaking rate (default 110 WPM), voice selection, interruptibility (stop speaking when user starts talking), queuing of responses.

title: Audio session & background handling (mobile)
description: Handle the trickiest mobile-specific challenge: audio sessions. iOS requires proper AVAudioSession category configuration (playAndRecord for simultaneous mic+speaker). Android needs foreground service for background audio. Must handle: phone calls interrupting session, app backgrounding (keep listening/speaking), notification sounds, Bluetooth headphones connecting/disconnecting, Do Not Disturb mode. This is the make-or-break reliability task.

title: Privacy/data-handling spec: retention toggle (no-retention default only for multi-user hosted)
description:
Spec how session data (intimate meditation transcripts) is retained, with privacy copy and per-provider disclosure.

**Key correction (2026-05-23): retention is a TOGGLE, and the default DEPENDS on deployment context — do NOT make ephemeral the global default.**
- **Local / single-user (desktop, on-device mobile):** keep session history by DEFAULT — this is the current behavior (sessions auto-save; there's a History view). It's the user's own device; retention is a feature they rely on. Per-session opt-out already exists conceptually as Private Session Mode (meditation-pal-ht8).
- **Multi-user hosted (the web demo / cloud backend):** default to NO server-side retention of transcripts (ephemeral). This is where storing strangers' intimate disclosures is a real risk. Still a toggle — a signed-in user could opt IN to saving their own history server-side if we offer accounts — but off by default.

So: one retention setting, context-aware default (local on / hosted off), surfaced clearly to the user.

**Also in scope:**
- Short, clear privacy statement.
- Per-provider data-flow disclosure: where audio/text actually goes (e.g. Web Speech API ships audio to Google; Groq/Anthropic API retention terms; on-device = nothing leaves the device).
- Tie the "nothing is stored" promise to the privacy-as-positioning angle (see related positioning work) — for hosted, prove it in the UI.

Relates to meditation-pal-ht8 (private session mode = the per-session toggle). Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.

title: Onboarding wizard for new users
description:
Guided first-run flow: LLM provider setup, voice selection with Piper recommendation, quality prompt. Currently a single settings page with a welcome header — could become a step-by-step wizard.

Mobile note (added 2026-05-09 by Claude): now that the form is mobile-shaped (responsive layout closed), a wizard implementation could lean into a 1-screen-per-decision flow on phone — intention → focus → vibe → directiveness → start, each occupying its own screen with the bottom nav reduced or replaced by Next/Back. Would feel native on phone and is straightforward to also render on desktop as either the same screens or an overview.

Mobile LLM tier picker (added 2026-05-09 by Claude): on the standalone mobile app, the onboarding LLM choice will be a 3-way pick rather than a provider list:
1. **On-device** (default, free, private)
2. **Use your own API key** (free, advanced)
3. **Cloud upgrade** ($4.99 pack or $3.99/mo)

The wizard should make on-device feel like a complete, real choice — not a trial. The cloud upgrade should be reachable in one tap but not pushed.

title: Handle concurrent Whisper transcription
description: Multiple simultaneous users will bottleneck on Whisper STT. Options: request queue, smaller model option, or OpenAI Whisper API fallback.

title: Private session mode (no history)
description:
Add a 'private session' toggle on the index page that, when checked, prevents the session from being written to history. Useful for sensitive sessions (grief, trauma, anything the user wants to leave no trace of).

Design notes (from end-of-day-2026-05-09 brainstorm with Claude):

- UI: a checkbox or toggle near the Begin Session button. Unchecked by default. Persistence preference (remember last value) is debatable — defaulting to off each session is safer than accidentally leaving 'private' on.
- Behavior: skip the writes that build the session JSON file at session end. The doEndSession flow already has a 'showSkipSave' branch for the End-Without-Saving button — private mode is essentially 'always skip save', set up-front instead of decided at the end.
- Conversation transcript should still display in the live UI; only the persistence is suppressed.

Related ergonomic improvement to bundle (suggested by Claude):

- For NON-private sessions, add periodic auto-saves (e.g. every 30s or after every assistant turn) so a user who sits with a long session doesn't lose it to a crash, browser close, or accidental reload. This is independent of private mode but spec'd here because private mode highlights the persistence story.

title: Implement speech-to-text (mobile)
description: Implement voice capture and transcription on mobile. Options to evaluate: (1) expo-speech-recognition or react-native-voice for platform-native STT (Apple Speech / Android SpeechRecognizer) - simplest, no model downloads, good accuracy. (2) whisper.cpp via react-native bridge for on-device Whisper - more control, works offline, but larger app size (~40-150MB for model). Recommendation: start with platform-native STT, add Whisper option later. Must handle: continuous listening, VAD/silence detection for turn boundaries, background audio interruptions, microphone permissions.

title: Build Setup screen (mobile)
description: Port the web setup page to a React Native screen. Includes: intention text input, 6 preset cards (Pleasant Play, Warmth & Goodwill, Parts Work, Somatic, Freeform, Stillness), attention focus multi-select (Body sensations, Emotions & feeling tone, Inner parts & inner world), facilitator quality multi-select (Playful, Compassionate, Loving, Spacious, Effortless), directiveness slider (0-10), orient-pleasant toggle, verbosity selector (low/medium/high), LLM provider/model picker, and custom instructions. Should feel native and meditative - clean, calm UI.

title: Mobile audio & Safari quirks
description:
Handle remaining mobile browser audio issues for reliable phone-as-remote.

Done:
- Wake Lock API for screen sleep prevention (src/web/static/js/wakelock.js)
- getUserMedia permissions flow (src/web/static/js/audio.js)

Remaining:
- Autoplay policy handling (TTS playback after backgrounding, first-tap unlock pattern)
- Safari WebSocket reconnection when the app is backgrounded / tab switched

Updated 2026-05-09 by Claude: scoped down after audit found Wake Lock and mic permissions are already implemented.

title: Web economics spreadsheet (no-app-store-tax variant of a2j)
description: The web-specific margin math. meditation-pal-a2j's spreadsheet assumes Apple/Google's 30% cut; web uses Stripe (~3%), which roughly doubles margin headroom and softens a2j's heavy-user trap. Model: per-session metered cost (LLM/STT/TTS) for median + heavy users across the candidate models (Gemini Flash-Lite, Groq small, Haiku 4.5, Sonnet), credit-pack price points, margin multiplier, and break-even. Output: final web price points + honest 'you're buying compute' copy spec (per a2j's no-'pay to feel calm' values guardrail). Extends meditation-pal-a2j.

title: Idea: multi-state orb (listening/thinking/speaking/holding visual modes)
description:
During the TS rewrite I prototyped a session orb with distinct visual modes for each conversation phase, then reverted to the existing app's breathing/holding states under the lift-first rule (see meditation-pal-cfy). User liked the idea and wants to revisit after parity work is done.

The prototype had:
- orb-listening: hue-rotate(-20deg), saturate(1.2), 1.4s pulse animation (transform + box-shadow grow)
- orb-thinking: saturate(0.5), 1.2s contracting pulse (subtle inhale)
- orb-speaking: brightness(1.15), 0.9s 3-keyframe ripple
- orb-holding: existing app's silence mode, but slower 9s breathe

Driven by setOrb(mode) calls in session.ts at:
- start of LLM thinking: setOrb('thinking')
- start of TTS playback: setOrb('speaking')
- HOLD signal received: setOrb('holding')
- mic active: setOrb('listening')
- idle: setOrb('idle') -> orb-breathing

Decision needed: do these augment the existing orb states (orb-breathing + orb-holding + orb-muted + orb-kasina + orb-rainbow), replace any, or live as their own mode set the user can opt into?

Original implementation lives in commit 1c14861 (later reverted in 29fc6d8) for reference.

title: Mobile responsive: active session UI + Wake Lock
description: During an active session on mobile, hide the bottom tab bar and most chrome. Show only orb, message area, and a hamburger that opens a sheet with End Session / New Session / History plus any in-session controls. Include Wake Lock API to keep the screen on during a session — without it, screen sleep breaks audio/WebSocket. Wake Lock is the one thing rolled in from meditation-pal-kgv since it's a usability blocker for mobile sessions. Part of meditation-pal-leo.

title: Responsive layout / mobile support
description: The app looks bad below 1024px. Native app now enforces min_size (1024, 600) but browser users can still resize smaller. Need responsive breakpoints, collapsible nav, and mobile-friendly layouts. Consider rolling in the nav flicker fix (SPA-style navigation) at the same time since both require structural changes to the page layout.

title: Pre-launch: protect the 'aloud.' brand (stylized mark, weak-word strategy)
description:
Protect the "aloud." brand before App Store launch. Name is FINAL (2026-05-23) — see context below; this ticket is about HOW to protect a deliberately-chosen weak word, not whether to reconsider it.

**Context / decisions:**
- The old name "glooow" and anything "glow"-adjacent is permanently dead: the meditation app GLO holds and actively enforces a trademark against similar-sounding names. Not a taste call — a legal one.
- "aloud." was chosen knowing it's a common dictionary word with weaker enforceability (descriptive for a voice app; likely descriptiveness refusal on the bare word). The user has explored alternatives; everything good is overloaded/taken. Decision stands.
- The moat is therefore TRUST + PRIVACY + PROMPT CRAFT, not brand-law. Trademark is defensive hygiene, not the strategy.

**Approach (given the weak word):**
- Pursue a STYLIZED wordmark / logo / composite mark (design + word) rather than the bare word "aloud" — stylization adds the distinctiveness the bare word lacks and is far more registrable.
- USPTO search in class 9 (software) + class 41 (services); expect to need the stylized mark to clear.
- File via TEAS (~$250-350); a TM attorney ($500-1500) is more clearly worth it here given descriptiveness risk — get a quick read on the stylized-mark path before filing.
- Common-law use protects the styled brand automatically to a degree; registration of the stylized mark is what enables App Store clone takedowns.
- Defer international (Madrid) until traction.

Spawned from 2026-05-09 license review; rebrand context added 2026-05-23. Loosely blocks meditation-pal-7rh (App Store submission).

title: Mobile responsive: history page
description: Make /history usable on phones. Should be small — single column already-ish. Part of meditation-pal-leo.

title: Build History screen (mobile)
description: Port the web history page to a React Native screen. Shows list of past sessions with date, duration, and intention. Tap to view full transcript (user and assistant turns). Swipe to delete. Store sessions in AsyncStorage or SQLite (expo-sqlite). Import/export session data as JSON for compatibility with the web app's session format.

title: Native mobile apps (iOS/Android)
description: Package glooow as native mobile apps. Depends on PWA manifest and responsive layout being done first.

title: Multi-model access via OpenRouter (power-user 'pick your model')
description: Optional: let credit-paying users choose among many models without us integrating each provider. OpenRouter gives one integration + unified billing across ~hundreds of models; its per-request cost feeds the metered billing engine. Surfaces a 'pick your model' option in the web settings for power users. Blocked by the standalone proxy + meditation-pal-2qg.

title: Web OAuth sign-in (Google/Apple) + account model
description: Add 'Sign in with Google/Apple/etc.' to the web demo using an off-the-shelf provider (Auth0/Clerk/Supabase Auth) — do not roll our own. Account identity is what gates the free-tier sessions and holds credit balances. This is the web analogue of the Apple/Google sign-in noted in meditation-pal-dbd, but via web OAuth rather than IAP. Foundation for free-tier gating and credits. Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.

title: Get VibeVoice TTS working end-to-end
description:
VibeVoice TTS is non-functional due to upstream packaging issues from Microsoft.

## What's broken

1. **Voice presets missing from HuggingFace repo.** The model's `generate()` requires `all_prefilled_outputs` (speaker embedding .pt files like `en-Emma_woman.pt`). The HF repo `microsoft/VibeVoice-Realtime-0.5B` only contains `config.json`, `model.safetensors`, `preprocessor_config.json` — no `voices/` directory. Without presets, synthesis fails with "Voice preset file not found."

2. **Architecture mismatch.** The HF checkpoint declares `model_type: vibevoice_streaming` and `architectures: [VibeVoiceStreamingForConditionalGenerationInference]`, but loading produces hundreds of "newly initialized" weight warnings — the streaming model architecture doesn't fully align with the checkpoint weights. Many `acoustic_tokenizer` and `semantic_tokenizer` layers are randomly initialized.

3. **PyPI package outdated.** PyPI has v0.0.1 without streaming classes. Must install from GitHub (`git+https://github.com/microsoft/VibeVoice.git`) for v1.0.0. The `[streamingtts]` extra doesn't exist.

## What we've already fixed

- Import paths updated to streaming class names
- `device_map` bypass for MPS (avoids "Cannot copy out of meta tensor")
- `is_model_downloaded()` now checks for voice presets, not just model weights
- Download endpoint uses model ID, not voice name
- Install command points to GitHub repo
- Voice list refreshes after download

## To resolve

Options in order of preference:
- **Wait for Microsoft to publish voice presets** to the HF repo and fix the architecture mismatch
- **Contact Microsoft** to ask about the presets — they may be gated or require a separate download
- **Check if a different model revision** or repo has the complete package
- **Disable VibeVoice in the UI** until it's actually functional (show "Coming soon" or similar)

title: Phase 5: Polish (pagination, auth, CSS, CI, updater)
Create dedicated aloud. web page
description:
Create a dedicated web page for aloud. to live on the existing personal site. Should be a snappy, visual presentation of what aloud. is — distilled from the README but punchier and more inviting.

Content to include:
- Hero/intro: what aloud. is in one sentence (voice-based meditation facilitator)
- Key features at a glance (AI facilitation, noting circles, multiple providers, local-first)
- Meditation styles overview — short descriptions of each style with room to expand (exploration, noting, etc.). These could double as content for in-app Explainers later.
- How it works / architecture overview (lightweight — not a docs page)
- Screenshots or visual previews
- Download / get started CTA

Tone: warm, approachable, not overly technical. Let the personality of the app come through.

title: Decide: Python backend vs TypeScript rewrite for mobile
description:
Decision ticket: should the standalone mobile app continue to talk to a Python (Flask + SocketIO) backend hosted somewhere, or should we rewrite the backend in TypeScript so it runs inside Capacitor as JS on the device?

Context from 2026-05-09 conversation:
- With native STT + native TTS + on-device small LLM, there is no inference work happening in the backend — it's pacing state machine, prompt assembly, session history, LLM API calls. ~Few hundred lines of orchestration.
- Capacitor cannot bundle Python; a Python backend implies a hosted service, which contradicts the 'no cloud, no account, all on device' value prop for the free tier.
- TypeScript rewrite estimate: 4–6 weeks. Rust would be ~3x more effort with marginal benefit since no on-device inference happens in user code.
- Desktop app (pywebview) currently depends on Python. If TS rewrite happens, desktop swaps to Electron / Tauri / Node sidecar, OR Python is kept as a parallel desktop-only implementation (maintenance cost).

Outcomes to choose between:
1. **Keep Python, host a backend for mobile** — fastest to Phase 2 ship, but commits to ongoing server costs and 'on-device' becomes a marketing half-truth.
2. **Rewrite in TS, retire Python** — bigger upfront cost, cleaner long-term story, unifies mobile + desktop runtime.
3. **Rewrite in TS, keep Python alongside for desktop CLI / power users** — flexible but doubles maintenance.

Probably defer until on-device LLM validation lands — if on-device LLM quality is good, option 2 becomes much more attractive (it's the only way to honestly ship 'all on-device'). If on-device LLM is mediocre and the cloud tier is doing the real work, option 1 is fine.

Spawned from 2026-05-09 mobile architecture conversation.

title: Implement LLM provider abstraction (mobile)
description: Create a TypeScript LLM client that supports the same providers as the Python app: Anthropic Claude (direct API), OpenAI, OpenRouter, and Ollama. Use fetch/axios for HTTP streaming. Implement the same context management (rolling window, max_tokens). Start with Anthropic as primary. The CLIProxyAPI provider can be dropped since mobile won't use homebrew Claude proxy.

title: Add basic authentication
description: Simple password gate or basic auth so only intended users can start sessions. Not needed while behind home NAT, but good hygiene before any port-forwarding or tunnel use.

title: Realize hosted LLM proxy as a standalone (non-Flask) service
description:
Build the actual deployable hosted proxy that meditation-pal-dbd specifies as a design. Today only a CLIENT shim exists (ts/ui/src/adapters/claude-proxy-http.ts) that POSTs to the local Flask /api/llm/claude_proxy/complete (desktop subscription routing) — there is no multi-user hosted backend.

Scope: a small stateless service that accepts completion requests from the ts/ui client, authenticates the user (see OAuth ticket), enforces per-user quota/credit debit (see billing ticket), forwards to the chosen provider (Anthropic / Groq / OpenRouter), and streams the response back. No Whisper, no Piper, no in-memory session state — sessions stay client-side. Deploy as the same proxy mobile will use (meditation-pal-dbd).

Extends meditation-pal-dbd. Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.

title: Scaffold Expo/React Native project
description: Initialize a new Expo project (managed workflow). Set up TypeScript, navigation (React Navigation), and basic folder structure mirroring the Python app: screens (Setup, Session, History), services (llm, stt, tts, audio), facilitation (prompts, pacing, session). Install core dependencies: expo-av, socket.io-client or direct HTTP, async storage.

title: Fix nav flicker on page navigation in pywebview
description: WKWebView (pywebview on macOS) clears the viewport between page navigations, causing a visible flash/repaint of the nav bar. Firefox doesn't exhibit this. Root cause is full-page navigation — WKWebView re-renders from scratch each time. Fix options: (1) SPA-style content swapping for static pages (index, history, settings) to avoid full navigations, or (2) investigate WKWebView compositing settings. Could be rolled into the responsive layout work since both touch the nav/page structure. The random CSS cache buster was already replaced with version-based caching which may have helped slightly.

title: Expand about page with more donation/contact options
description: Add more ways to donate/support beyond current options. Include: email contact, cal.com calendar link for scheduling time. Make it easy for people to reach out.

title: Prompt cache persistence: 1h / adaptive TTL for long meditation gaps
description:
Prompt caching tuning for the Anthropic LLM provider. Today the only cache breakpoint is `cache_control: {type: 'ephemeral'}` on the system prompt in ts/src/llm/anthropic.ts (buildRequest), which uses the DEFAULT 5-MINUTE TTL. There is no 1h TTL, no cache-miss detection, and the cache_read/creation usage fields are declared in claude-proxy.ts but never read.

WHY THIS MATTERS FOR MEDITATION: pacing.ts sets silenceCheckinSec=300, and those check-ins are CANNED phrases, not LLM calls. So a meditator can share, then sit silent for 10-20 min before the next utterance triggers an LLM call. That gap routinely exceeds 5 min — exactly when the ephemeral cache dies and the next call repays a fresh cache write instead of a cheap read. Meditation is close to a worst case for the 5-min default.

ECONOMICS (from Anthropic prompt-caching docs): 5-min write = 1.25x base input, 1h write = 2x, cache read = 0.1x. Each cache READ refreshes the TTL back to its original duration, so a 1h entry stays alive across any gap under an hour. For N LLM calls spaced 5min-1h apart: flat-5min costs 1.25N; flat-1h costs 2 + 0.1(N-1). 1h wins as soon as N>=2 — which a real session almost always hits. So unconditional 1h is the favorable default for this app.

OPTIONS:
A) Unconditional ttl:'1h' on the system-prompt breakpoint. One-line change, matches meditation's long-pause reality. RECOMMENDED default.
B) Adaptive TTL: detect a cache miss (cache_read_input_tokens==0 when a hit was expected) and flip the breakpoint to ttl:'1h' after X misses. The principled version. Needs plumbing — anthropic.ts currently discards the cache usage fields (it only sums input+output), so CompletionResult must carry cache_read/creation tokens. Spares quick chatty sessions the 2x write premium.
C) Fixed clock cadence (e.g. write a 1h cache on the first hit after each 10-min mark, 5-min otherwise). Fiddly, and only helps in the narrow band where gaps hover right around 5 min. NOT recommended given the break-even math above.

CAVEATS / BLOCKERS:
1) TTL control exists ONLY on the direct Anthropic API path. The default subscription path (claude-proxy.ts, shelling out to the `claude` CLI) cannot set cache_control/TTL at all — caching there is internal to Claude Code. So any of this only bites when the user runs with their own ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
2) Only the system prompt is cached, NOT the growing conversation — and if that prompt is under the model's minimum cacheable size (~2048 tokens on Sonnet 4.6, 4096 on Opus) it silently won't cache regardless of TTL. Confirm the rendered PromptBuilder output clears the floor; otherwise TTL is moot and the bigger win is adding a breakpoint on the conversation prefix (last block of the latest turn).

VERIFY: check response usage.cache_read_input_tokens across turns; zero across repeated same-prefix requests means a silent invalidator or sub-minimum prefix.

Cost relevance: this is a direct knob on hosted-tier per-session cost — see meditation-pal-a2j and meditation-pal-dbd (cost math), meditation-pal-8sj (per-request cost engine), meditation-pal-14s (cost meter). Lands in the Anthropic provider from meditation-pal-2qg (TS orchestration core).

(Filed 2026-05-25 by Claude from a design discussion.)
